title: Alabama's Nitrogen Execution Is at the Supreme Court Tonight
script: Is nitrogen execution legal? Jeffery Lee has been on Alabama's death row for over 2 decades after being convicted of a 1998 double murder. Alabama scheduled his execution for June 11 — tonight — by nitrogen hypoxia: a gas mask forces pure nitrogen into the lungs until the condemned person dies. But on June 9, US District Judge Emily C. Marks ruled the method amounts to cruel and unusual punishment, citing the severe air hunger — the desperate gasping — that witnesses reported in earlier executions. Alabama rushed to the US Supreme Court asking them to overturn the ruling and allow tonight's execution. The Supreme Court hasn't ruled yet. One more twist: the original jury voted 7 to 5 for life in prison — a judge overrode that verdict. Will the Supreme Court let Alabama execute Jeffery Lee by nitrogen tonight?
